codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and current code paths for the Claude platform list UI changes. The implementation appears to satisfy the assigned list-surface requirements: one Claude platform still aggregates both sources, duplicate rows remain visible, each row shows source origin, marketplace rows are visibly read-only and lose list-level install affordances, user rows remain actionable, and search now preserves duplicate-source matches by checking skill id as well as name and description.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature ships new user-visible UI strings via `t(..., { defaultValue })` in the component instead of adding locale entries. AGENTS/CLAUDE guidance says user-visible text must go through i18n, but shared state does not clarify whether default-only strings without `en.json`/`zh.json` keys are acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/skill/UnifiedSkillCard.tsx:392 uses `platform.sourceNativeLabel`; :429 uses `platform.originMarketplace`; :432 uses `platform.originUser`; :445 uses `platform.readOnly`. Searching `src/i18n/locales/**/*.json` found no matching locale keys.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Native Tauri UI verification on macOS currently relies on ad-hoc accessibility and screenshot commands that are not captured in `.factory/services.yaml` or mission library docs, so future workers may have to rediscover the same procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript skeleton for worker session `96e7c543-5b70-4ba4-af36-37eed50938f3` shows repeated `Execute` calls using `osascript`, `screencapture`, and accessibility-tree inspection after starting `HOME=/tmp/skills-manage-test-fixtures/claude-multi-source pnpm tauri dev`, while `.factory/services.yaml` documents only start/stop/healthcheck for `tauri-dev-isolated`.

summary: Pass. I found no blocking or non-blocking code defects in scope for this feature review. The main triage items are shared-state/documentation gaps around locale-key expectations for defaulted UI strings and the undocumented macOS native-validation workflow.
